registration.registered_by: docs/OPH_FALSIFICATION_PROGRAM.md, forward W/Z note: the two-loop transport repair executed 2026-07-16 and is excluded; CL-5 resolution moves to the radiative pole packet, which this document specifies
registration.predecessor_artifacts_frozen: the 2026-07-14 sweep and the 2026-07-16 two-loop artifacts are read-only inputs; this computation edits neither them nor any one-loop lane module
registration.scope_study: code/particles/calibration/EW_POLE_PACKET_SCOPE_2026-07-16.md, written before this spec; it maps which on-shell packet pieces apply to the declared chain and which its conventions absorb

chain_modules: code/particles/calibration/legacy_d10/particle_masses_paper_d10_d11.py (build_paper_d10, run_alphas_from_unification, v_from_transmutation, pixel_residual, unification_scale_gev, mz_tree_from_v_and_couplings, integrate_d11_literal_core)
baseline_one_loop_chain.tree_truncation_site: the readout is the tree two-law evaluation of the running couplings at mz_run and the transmutation vev; the propagator pole shift Re Sigma_hat_T is the omitted packet

engine.reference: A. Denner, Fortschr. Phys. 41 (1993) 307, arXiv:0709.1075: appendix B gauge-boson self-energies Sigma^W_T, Sigma^ZZ_T, Sigma^AZ_T, Sigma^AA_T ('t Hooft-Feynman gauge, tadpole-renormalized scheme hat T = 0 per sect. 3.3 eq. (RCT)); appendix A couplings g_f^+ = -(s_W/c_W) Q_f, g_f^- = (I3_f - s_W^2 Q_f)/(s_W c_W); sect. 4.4 A_0 and B_0 (eq. (B0)); sect. 8 Delta r used as certification target only
engine.sign_convention: sect. 3.3: Gamma_T(k^2) = -i (k^2 - M^2) - i Sigma_T(k^2), so M_pole^2 = m^2(mu) - Re Sigma_hat_T(m^2; mu) with Sigma_hat_T the MS-bar finite part (Delta -> 0) at scale mu
engine.certification: validate() executed 2026-07-16 before this spec was written: Sigma^AA_T(0) = 0 exactly; Delta r mu-independent to 3e-17; the Veltman m_t^2 term of Sigma^ZZ_T(0)/M_Z^2 - Sigma^W_T(0)/M_W^2 reproduced to 2e-15 relative; Denner's sect. 8 input set reproduces his quoted M_W = 80.23 GeV to 1.2 MeV through the resummed Delta r relation (linear form 80.262); closed-form B_0 matches Feynman-parameter quadrature to 3.5e-6; all_ok true. Measured-world numbers appear in the harness only and never feed the chain

revision_declaration.summary: the tree two-law readout is completed by the one-loop propagator pole shift: per cell, MV_pole^2 = MV_tree^2 - Re Sigma_hat_T(MV_tree^2; mz_run) with V in {W, Z}; every transport object, the pixel-residual selector formula, the transmutation law, the eta_source reduction, and the clock display adapter are retained exactly
revision_declaration.self_energy_parameter_point_per_cell: sw2 = sin2w_eff of the cell's law output; cw2 = 1 - sw2; MW, MZ inside the loops = the cell's tree masses in display GeV; MH = mH_stage5; alpha = a2p_eff * sw2 with a2p_eff = (MW_tree/E_star)^2 / (pi (v/E_star)^2); leptons and the first two quark generations massless; CKM = identity; top mass per branch
revision_declaration.evaluation_point: self-energies evaluated at k^2 = MV_tree^2; MS-bar scale mu = the basis fixed-point scale mz_run (baseline mz_run for tier A, re-solved mz_run for tier B)
revision_declaration.internal_input_policy: Stage-5 masses only, never PDG: loop-internal top and Higgs masses come from integrate_d11_literal_core(build_paper_d10(P_sl3)) of the same chain
revision_declaration.zero_tunable_elements: this revision contains zero tunable elements; every scale, boundary, grid, window, branch, and the primary cell below are fixed in this document before any pole-packet chain number exists, and every declared cell is emitted

tier.rule: the mZ fixed point is re-declared on the pole mass: mu solves MZ_pole(mu) = mu, where MZ_pole(mu)^2 = MZ_tree(mu)^2 - Re Sigma_hat_ZZ(MZ_tree(mu)^2; mu) on the plain (a2, aY) tuple of the chain's fixed-point object (sw2_fp = aY/(a2+aY), MW_fp = v sqrt(pi a2), alpha_fp = a2 sw2_fp, frozen Stage-5 mt and mH); alpha_U is then re-solved so that pixel_residual(a2(mz_run), a3(mz_run), P) = 0 at the new fixed point, and v/E_star is recomputed from the selected alpha_U; this is the full self-consistent absorption of the packet
alpha_U, mu_U, mz_run, and v/E_star are held at the frozen baseline; only the readout masses receive the pole shift; this isolates the certified packet size on the emitted pair

mt_loop.rule: the Stage-5 top pole mass 164.13057624202088 GeV enters the loops
the Stage-5 MS-bar top mass 154.77925772233598 GeV enters the loops

discrete_branch_menu.z_scheme_retired: the z axis of the predecessor menus is retired inside this lane: the z_stage3 divisor sqrt(1 + 3/(32 pi^2)) is a partial rho-type piece of the same self-energy packet, and carrying it alongside the full Re Sigma_ZZ double-counts; all cells are z_tree (gate PP-03)
discrete_branch_menu.primary_display_cell: tier=B_pole_fixed_point|mt=mt_pole_stage5|law=zero_selector (same law as the baseline chain key)
discrete_branch_menu.cells_emitted: all 8 combinations; none is selected after results exist

numerical_settings_fixed_before_computat.fixed_point_solver: mirror of solve_mz_fixed_point_tree of the lane: 260-point logspace grid over [1e0, 1e5] GeV, sign-change bracketing, 90 geometric-midpoint bisection iterations; grid points where MZ_pole^2 <= 0 are recorded as invalid and never bracket

alpha_U_root_finding.bracket_scan: 41 equally spaced points, mirroring solve_alpha_u_from_p of the lane
alpha_U_root_finding.memoization: residual values are cached per alpha_U bit pattern; cached evaluation is exact re-reading, never approximation
alpha_U_root_finding.failure_rule: fail closed; if no sign change exists in the window the tier B branch is gated with the scan recorded, mirroring the frozen sweep failure protocol

numerical_settings_fixed_before_computat.pole_shift_failure_rule: fail closed; any cell with MV_tree^2 - Re Sigma_hat_T <= 0 is gated and reported, never substituted
numerical_settings_fixed_before_computat.precision: float64 throughout; the two-law evaluator uses its own scoped internals unchanged; no module-level mpmath state is touched

outputs.landing_criterion: MW inside 80.3692 +- 0.0133 GeV AND MZ inside 91.1880 +- 0.0020 GeV simultaneously, identical to the frozen menu
outputs.direction_verdict_rule: evaluated on the primary display cell only: toward_measurement if both |MW_offset| and |MZ_offset| shrink relative to the baseline zero_selector z_tree cell, away_from_measurement if both grow, mixed otherwise; toward supports the missing-correction reading of CL-5, away kills the pole-packet branch as declared here

fail_closed_gates_declared_open: GATE-PP-01 tadpole_fj_branch_uncertified: the Fleischer-Jegerlehner vev-convention alternative is declared, uncomputed, and gated; the packet is defined in the reference's tadpole-renormalized scheme (hat T = 0), which matches the chain's use of v as the actual vacuum value; the FJ alternative differs by mt^4/MH^2-enhanced terms
GATE-PP-02 higher_order_pole_terms: O(alpha alpha_s) and two-loop electroweak pole terms uncomputed
GATE-PP-03 z_stage3_superseded: the stage-3 divisor is retired inside this lane to prevent rho double-counting
GATE-PP-04 stage5_loop_masses_frozen: internal (mt, mH) frozen at baseline values inside the tier B re-solve; their alpha_U dependence enters the packet at two-loop order
GATE-PP-05 transmutation_vev_scheme_open: v carries no declared loop-order completion; read as the scheme parameter at mz_run
GATE-PP-06 pole_point_iteration: self-energies evaluated at the tree mass point; iteration to the pole is second order
GATE-PP-07 w_fixed_point_absent: the chain declares a Z fixed point only; M_W receives its pole shift at the readout and enters no scale condition
rules_of_record: no scale, scheme, or branch choice is made after any result exists; the menu above is closed and every cell is emitted
the one-loop lane modules, the frozen 2026-07-14 artifacts, and the 2026-07-16 two-loop artifacts are never edited
a landing or a direction verdict on the primary cell counts the discrete selections declared here and closes nothing by itself

kill-condition protocol: if the result is adverse in every cell and framework-threatening, the transcription, sign conventions, scheme consistency, and baseline reproduction are audited against the declared references before reporting; the artifact records what the computation produced either way

spec_sha256.scope: computed over the canonical serialization of this document with the spec_sha256 field removed
